page: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions (AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16). The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8a.8xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for single-core CPU execution, single-threaded gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores also rank in the top 10%, alongside image and text processing tasks. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth (reaching 213.23 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads), and software compilation with Clang fall within the top 25%. Database operations, measured via Passmark, rank in the top 10%, while Redis SET operations achieve a strong rating with 106.65 million operations per second.

While the instance excels at small-to-medium language model inference, achieving top-tier token generation speeds with a 135M model and strong speeds with a 7B model, its performance drops to average when processing large 70B models. Additionally, the complete absence of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, high clock speed, and strong memory bandwidth, the m8a.8xlarge is suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and moderate machine learning inference workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, delivering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark data places the instance in the top 10% for single-core CPU performance, database operations, and small-model LLM inference, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth rank in the top 25%. However, performance drops to average for large 70B LLM inference. This hardware profile makes the instance highly effective for database management, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing workloads where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core and database performance without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC instance with DDR5 memory, delivering top-tier single-core performance and balanced resources for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in database operations and single-core benchmarks
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database management, web serving, and software compilation
